calendar.matters:
Petition To Approve Amendment To The Bypass Trust Of The Najarro Family Trust To Confirm To The Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Feb-28-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Victoria Sood for Adela Najarro.

The Court stated that no objection had been filed. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Brown.

Petition To Approve Amendment To The Bypass Trust Of The Najarro Family Trust To Confirm To The Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Feb-08-2011 Continued To Feb-28-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Motion For Judgment Of Dismissal And Award Of Attorney Fees And Expenses To Lillian M. Najarro Following Order After Hearing; Points And Authorities; Supporting Declaration Of Richard A Canatella; Request For Judicial Notice; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Oct-15-2009 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For Sanctions For Filing Frivolous Petition To Ascertain Beneficiaries Based On Frivolous Claim That Lillian M. Najarro And Danilo Najarro Violated The No Contest Clause In The Najarro Family Trust; Points And Authorities; Request For Judicial Notice; Proof Of Mail Service
Off Calendar Oct-15-2009 Off Calendar

Petition To Ascertain Beneficiaries Of Trust
Off Calendar Aug-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PES-08-291473.

Appearances: Victoria Tran Sood for Adela Najarro. Richard Canatella for and with Lillian Najarro (Petitioner).

The Court stated that Mr. Canatella had filed an objection and that it was liening towards a ruling that William and Donald are beneficiaries and they have not violated the no contest clause. Ms. Tran Sood stated that this matter was set for status and that she thought there was to be an evidentiary hearing on the will contest. Mr. Canatella stated that this Court heard a matter last week and that the no contest clause doesn't reach a settlor. Ms. Tran Sood argued that the only issue on last week was the Anti-Slapp issue. The Court stated that it was prepared to go forward. Ms. Tran Sood stated that she should be able to call witnesses and that she wasn't noticed that this would be argued today. The Court stated that Adela Najarro's petition to probate the will is to be set for an evidentiary hearing and that Lillian Najarro's petitions for will contest and spousal property would trail. Ms. Tran Sood stated that all the petitions should be tried together, and informed the Court that Mr. Canatella had just filed an appeal. The matters were taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Murphy.

Petition To Ascertain Beneficiaries Of Trust
Off Calendar Aug-10-2009 Continued To Aug-17-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Motion To Strike Petition To Ascertain Beneficiaries; Points And Authorities; Request For Judicial Notice With Exhibits
Off Calendar Aug-06-2009 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella for and with Lillian Najarro. Victoria Tran Sood for Adela Najarro.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. Motion denied. Petitioner Adela Najarro's request for sanctions is denied. Ms. Tran Sood is to prepare the order and circulate it to Mr. Canatella for approval as to form.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
